title: Chapter 721
content:
Chapter 721: Catching Fire (R-18)

Strax raised an eyebrow, his crooked smile widening as if he’d just won the top prize in his favorite game: provoking Scarlet until she was about to explode.

"What is it?" he asked, his voice low, warm, and cheeky. "You turn all red when you’re jealous. It’s funny."

Scarlet narrowed her eyes slowly.

Dangerous.

Beautiful.

Deliciously ready to bite.

"I’m not jealous."

"Of course not," he murmured, leaning in a little closer until his breath brushed her neck. "You’re just growling because I mentioned another woman’s body. Coincidence."

"The problem is you, Strax!" she retorted, poking his chest with her finger, too irritated to realize—or admit—how much she was reacting. "You talk with that face, you use that tone, you act like that..."

"Like that...?" he repeated, lifting his chin slightly. She frowned.

And then, without warning, she grabbed his collar and pulled hard.

"Like this, exactly like this, you—"

Strax laughed mid-pull, letting himself fall back onto the bed without resistance. He didn’t need to—Scarlet was already climbing on top of him, as if it were where she truly belonged. Her thighs fit against the sides of his hips with an irritating, dangerous... intimate naturalness.

She wasn’t just riding him.

She was reigning over him.

Strax put his hands behind his head, deliberately provoking him.

"Ah. So you’re not jealous..." He slid his gaze up and down her body, brazenly.

"...but you’re climbing on me like you’re trying to mark your territory."

"Shut up."

"No."

He smiled.

"This is too much fun."

Scarlet pressed her fingers against his chest, firm enough to make him let out a soft sigh.

"You’re impossible."

"I know."

He lifted his hips slightly, just to remind her exactly where she was sitting.

"And you love it."

She opened her mouth to protest, but nothing came out.

Nothing.

Only the blush rising rapidly on her cheeks—the one she always tried to hide by turning her face away.

Strax chuckled softly.

"Look at that... even the tip of your nose is hot. You’re a walking flame."

"Strax, I swear on everything that’s sacred—"

"Are you going to hit me?"

He raised an eyebrow, cheeky.

"Are you going to punish me for mentioning Mercedes? For teasing you a little?"

Scarlet leaned over him, her hair falling like red curtains around both their faces. Her nose almost touched his. Her warm breath was practically a kiss without the final touch.

"You always tease me," she whispered, her voice heavy, almost hoarse. "And you know what’s worse?"

"What?"

"I let you."

Strax slid his hand along the side of her thigh—slow, lazy, utterly aware of the effect he had on her.

"Ah. Now we’re getting close to the truth."

"Shut up."

"I never shut up."

She pushed him against the mattress harder, almost as if trying to crush his mood—but his smile only widened.

"You’re being a bastard, Strax."

"Why?" he asked, feigning innocence. "For saying you’re jealous? Or for liking it?"

Scarlet narrowed her eyes.

"Because you know what it does to me."

"And what does it do?"

She bit her lower lip, as if trying to hold back too many words.

Then she responded with her body.

Slowly—too slowly—she slid her hip over his. Not enough to be indecent. But enough to leave him breathless for a second.

Strax inhaled through his nose, too focused to laugh this time.

"Scarlet..."

"You want to know what this does to me?" she murmured, her face pressed against his. "It makes me want to..."

Her hand moved up his chest, reaching his neck—gentle at first, then with the dangerous delicacy of someone who could squeeze but chooses not to.

"...stay like this all day."

Strax swallowed hard, his smile fading for a second.

Not because he was uncomfortable.

But because she had hit exactly where it had an effect.

He placed his hands on her waist, pulling her down a little closer.

"Ah... so that’s it."

"Yes."

"You want to sit on top of me all day."

"Yes."

The word came out without hesitation.

Without shame.

Without gentleness.

Strax laughed—a low, hoarse, almost dangerous laugh.

"And you say I’m the one who’s a jerk."

"You are."

"You too."

She smiled for the first time—that smile of hers, short, sharp, too beautiful.

Strax held her hip more firmly, but without moving her.

Not yet.

"Then why don’t you stay?" he murmured, his voice almost an invitation, almost a challenge.

Scarlet brought her mouth closer to his until their lips almost touched—almost.

"Because, if I stay on top of you all day..."

Strax felt her breath against his mouth.

"...you won’t be able to walk tomorrow."

He smiled slowly.

That smile of his.

The smile that could set the world on fire.

"Promise?" he whispered.

She laughed—warm, low, delicious.

And pulled his hair.

"Strax..."

"Hm?"

"Shut up."

Then, with the same hand that still held her waist, he grabbed her chin and pulled her into a strong, hungry kiss, enough to make them both lose their breath for a second.
